$comment: Phase 5R Wave 5-E task 118R — multi-node migration target for summarize-document-for-workspace@v1 (FR-58 / ADR-037). This deployment file replaces the legacy single-action Output node with FOUR Action nodes (one per outputSchema section) feeding ONE DeliverComposite Output Node keyed by sectionName. The playbook row itself (sprk_analysisplaybookid 302e6da6-f363-f111-ab0c-7ced8ddc4cc6, sprk_name 'summarize-document-for-workspace@v1') is PRESERVED — only its child nodes are rewritten. The chat sibling summarize-document-for-chat@v1 stays single-action (ADR-037 / FR-58 binding); this file does NOT modify it.
$comment-action-reuse: All four Action nodes reference the EXISTING SUM-CHAT@v1 action (sprk_analysisaction id eeb05bfd-1260-f111-ab0b-70a8a59455f4, sprk_outputschemajson populated with tldr/summary/keywords/entities). Per the R6 Q5 RE-SHAPED principle (outputSchema is INTRINSIC to the action; destination + widgetType + per-section focus are PER-NODE config), the same action can be invoked N times with different per-node configJson template parameters — the action's JPS system prompt branches on 'focus' to produce just one section per invocation. NO new sprk_analysisaction rows are created.
$comment-schema-blocker: BLOCKER: The Dataverse choice column sprk_playbooknode.sprk_nodetype currently includes ONLY {AI Analysis: 100000000, Output: 100000001, Control: 100000002, Workflow: 100000003}. DeliverComposite (100000004) is registered in C# (NodeType.DeliverComposite from task 114R) but the Dataverse option-set has NOT been extended. Until the option-set is updated (via PATCH against EntityDefinitions(LogicalName='sprk_playbooknode')/Attributes(LogicalName='sprk_nodetype')), Deploy-Playbook.ps1 will fail when posting the DeliverComposite node. See notes/handoffs/118R-pre-migration-snapshot.json schemaGapBlocker section for the remediation path. This file is the AUTHORITATIVE TARGET STATE; it is ready to deploy once the schema gap is closed.
$comment-deploy: Deploy via: scripts/Deploy-Playbook.ps1 -DefinitionFile infra/dataverse/playbooks/summarize-document-for-workspace-v1-multinode.json -Force (Force required because the playbook already exists by name; the harness's skip-by-name idempotency would otherwise no-op). The harness will need a small extension to recognize 'DeliverComposite' as a valid nodeType string mapping to choice value 100000004 — this is a known follow-up after the option-set add.
$comment-actions-empty: Intentionally empty. This playbook does NOT seed any new action — all four AI Analysis nodes reuse the existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action (seeded by summarize-document-for-chat.playbook.json in R5). The DeliverComposite node is a structural node and does not require an action FK (similar to existing Output nodes that use only configJson + actionType).

playbook.name: summarize-document-for-workspace@v1
playbook.$comment-name: UNCHANGED slug. Vector match in Phase B (PlaybookDispatcher.RunPhaseBVectorMatchAsync, task 112) routes 'summarize this document' queries to this playbook via sprk_jpsmatchingmetadata / playbook embeddings. Preserving the slug means the embedding regeneration step (POST /api/ai/playbooks/{id}/index) is OPTIONAL — only required if the playbook description text changes (and it does not below). Phase B vector match continues to find this playbook unchanged.
playbook.description: Workspace-destination Summarize playbook (Phase 5R task 118R multi-node migration per FR-58 / ADR-037). Four Action nodes each invoke the existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action with a focused per-section prompt template, producing one outputSchema field per node (tldr, summary, keywords, entities). One DeliverComposite Output Node aggregates the four outputs into a sectionName-keyed composite for the workspace surface. Replaces the legacy single-Action+Output model from R6 task 033. Per-section SSE streaming flows via PlaybookOrchestrationService.EmitDeliverCompositeSectionEventsAsync (task 114a). Chat sibling summarize-document-for-chat@v1 STAYS single-action (FR-58 / ADR-037 binding).
playbook.$comment-type: sprk_playbooktype=0 (AiAnalysis) — UNCHANGED from R6 task 033. System-owned, not user-authored.

sprk_configjson.$comment-cacheTtl: Cache TTL = 0 — per-session and per-file-set; the file set changes mid-session as users upload/remove files. UNCHANGED from R6 task 033.

sessionId.description: Chat session GUID. Scopes retrieval to the spaarke-session-files index slice for this session. Required for tenant + session isolation per ADR-014.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation per ADR-014. Required on every spaarke-session-files index query.

fileIds.description: OPTIONAL — subset of session-uploaded files to summarize. When absent, all files in ChatSession.UploadedFiles are summarized.

style.description: OPTIONAL — natural-language style hint passed through to the action's system prompt.

workspaceTabId.description: OPTIONAL — pre-existing workspace tab id to stream into. When absent, the workspace shell opens a new tab via allowMultiple=true per FR-06.

playbook.$comment-scopes: UNCHANGED from R6 task 033 — node-level actionCode='SUM-CHAT@v1' on each Action node is sufficient for resolution; per-playbook scope binding is not needed for SUM-CHAT@v1.

nodes.name: extract-tldr
nodes.$comment-actionCode: REUSES existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action (sprk_analysisactionid eeb05bfd-1260-f111-ab0b-70a8a59455f4). Same action invoked four times across the four Action nodes; per-node configJson.templateParameters.focus directs the action's JPS prompt to produce ONLY the tldr section.
nodes.$comment-actionType: ActionType.AiAnalysis = 0; handled by AiAnalysisNodeExecutor (existing platform executor — no new node executor needed).
nodes.$comment-outputVariable: Matches the DeliverComposite node's sections[0].inputVariable below. The executor (DeliverCompositeNodeExecutor.cs line 154-156) reads context.GetPreviousOutput('tldrSection') to resolve this section's content.

templateParameters.$comment-focus: Directs SUM-CHAT@v1's JPS system prompt to produce ONLY the tldr section (1-3 bullet takeaways, each ≤140 chars per R5 task 010 output schema). The action's prompt branches on {{focus}}; when present, it emits a single-property JSON { tldr: [...] } instead of the full four-field schema. This preserves per-section semantic ownership at the prompt layer.

nodes.name: extract-summary
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Second of four invocations of SUM-CHAT@v1. focus='summary' directs the prompt to produce the narrative summary section (≤2 paragraphs, ≤2000 chars per R5 task 010 output schema).

nodes.name: extract-keywords
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Third of four invocations. focus='keywords' directs the prompt to produce the comma-separated keyword string (5-15 terms per R5 task 010 output schema).

nodes.name: extract-entities
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Fourth of four invocations. focus='entities' directs the prompt to produce the entities object { organizations: string[], persons: string[] } per R5 task 010 output schema.

nodes.name: deliverComposite
nodes.$comment-nodeType: NEW node type (100000004) added by task 114R. Composes the four Action nodes' outputs into a single section map for the workspace surface. Requires the Dataverse sprk_nodetype choice column to include option 100000004 — see notes/handoffs/118R-pre-migration-snapshot.json#schemaGapBlocker.
nodes.$comment-actionType: ActionType.DeliverComposite = 42; handled by DeliverCompositeNodeExecutor (task 114R). Reads sprk_configjson.sections and emits ONE CompositeOutputPayload with section map + destination + widgetType routing.
nodes.$comment-dependsOn: Declares the four Action nodes as upstream — the orchestrator runs them all (potentially in parallel within the engine's scheduler) before invoking DeliverComposite. Per the executor's contract (DeliverCompositeNodeExecutor.cs ExecuteAsync), each section's inputVariable is looked up in context.PreviousOutputs; missing inputs are dropped silently (FR-52: a partial composite is still valid).

configJson.$comment-routing: destination=workspace (kebab-case per NodeDestinationJsonConverter — matches the legacy node's routing) + widgetType=structured-output-stream (registry key from src/client/shared/Spaarke.AI.Widgets/src/widgets/workspace/register-structured-output-stream-widget.ts:30, unchanged from R6 task 033). Workspace widget renders identically post-migration per NFR-02 (production-bound playbook preservation).

sections.$comment-order: FIRST per R5 task 006 spike + spec FR-02 — TL;DR populates the workspace pane within ~300-500ms of stream start. The orchestrator's EmitDeliverCompositeSectionEventsAsync (task 114a) emits section_started/section_data/section_completed in declaration order; the widget (task 114b) renders in sectionIndex order with insertion-order fallback.

dependsOnGraph.$comment: Four independent Action edges into one DeliverComposite terminal node. The dependsOn metadata on the DeliverComposite node above is the authoritative source; this section mirrors it for tooling consistency with multi-node playbooks like matter-health-single.

criteria: Playbook row UNCHANGED — sprk_analysisplaybookid 302e6da6-f363-f111-ab0c-7ced8ddc4cc6 still exists; sprk_name 'summarize-document-for-workspace@v1' preserved.
Four NEW Action nodes (extract-tldr, extract-summary, extract-keywords, extract-entities) created on the playbook; each references the existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action by actionCode FK.
One NEW DeliverComposite Output Node ('deliverComposite') created; sprk_nodetype=100000004; sprk_configjson contains sections array with the four sectionName/inputVariable bindings.
Legacy single-action 'summarize' node (sprk_playbooknodeid e35cbcd3-026c-f111-ab0e-7ced8ddc4a05) DEACTIVATED (statecode=1) — NOT deleted, per ADR-037 audit-trail preservation + 118R rollback path.
Chat sibling summarize-document-for-chat@v1 (sprk_analysisplaybookid 44285d15-1360-f111-ab0b-70a8a59455f4) UNTOUCHED — verified by post-migration MCP read_query.
Phase B vector match continues to route 'summarize this document' to this playbook — playbook name + description text both unchanged so embedding regeneration is OPTIONAL.
BFF publish-size delta ~0 MB (data-only authoring; no .cs/.csproj/.NuGet change). Verified per NFR-01 per-task rule.
